description:
You are working as a small-utility developer.  
  Inside /home/user/utils there is already a text file named “input.txt”.  
  Your goal is to produce a SHA-256 checksum record for that file and store the
  result in a **new** log file called  
  /home/user/utils/checksums.log  

  Output format requirements that the automated tests will check:

  1. /home/user/utils/checksums.log must contain **exactly one line**.  
  2. That line must be the *default* output of the command  
     “sha256sum input.txt” (i.e. digest, two blank spaces, filename).  
  3. The line must therefore look exactly like this (including the two spaces
     separating the digest and the filename and a single trailing newline):  

     2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824␠␠input.txt\n  

     – where “␠␠” represents two space characters and “\n” is the final newline.  
  4. No extra spaces, lines, or characters are allowed before or after.  

  Constraints & hints for the agent:

  • Only one simple terminal command is needed; do not open the file manually.  
  • The working directory can be chosen freely, but the entry in the log must
    reference the basename “input.txt” (not a relative or absolute path).  
  • If the log file already exists, overwrite it rather than appending.  

  When you are done, /home/user/utils/checksums.log should meet the exact
  format above so that the automated tests can validate your work.
truth:
Initial environment:
    • Directory /home/user/utils exists and is writable by the agent.
    • /home/user/utils/input.txt exists with the following exact content
      (5 bytes, no trailing newline): “hello”

  SHA-256 of that exact 5-byte string is:
    2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824

  Expected final state:
    • A file /home/user/utils/checksums.log exists, mode 644 (rw-r--r--),
      containing exactly these 66 characters, followed by a single 0x0A byte:

      2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824␠␠input.txt\n

      (Digest = 64 hex digits, two 0x20 spaces, “input.txt”, newline)

  The test suite will:
    1. Read /home/user/utils/checksums.log
    2. Verify it is one line long and matches the string above byte-for-byte.
name: task_000000_bf8fd1e7
